rationale: The ASMR-satisfaction emotional pitch and clean 3D-render aesthetic align well with Sand Loop's calm/satisfying tone and casual 25-45 audience, so the audience overlap is real. However, the palette (muted pinks/red) clashes with the game's bright sky-blue/white/multi-saturated identity, and the spice-jar pour hook fundamentally misrepresents the core loop — there's no conveyor belt, no color-sorting decision, no pixel-art reveal payoff. Recommend only as inspiration: borrow the satisfying-pour 3D framing but rebuild around multi-color sand pouring into pixel-art reveals to avoid misleading-ad install churn.
